items.briefing:
Security researchers have identified 58 critical software vulnerabilities (CVEs) that pose significant supply chain risks to organisations — meaning attackers could compromise your business through software or services you rely on from third-party vendors. This is a timely reminder for Australian organisations to keep all software updated and check in with key suppliers about their security practices.

On the local front, cybersecurity vendor Coro has signed an Australian distribution deal through Leader, expanding access to its security tools for local businesses. Darwinium has also updated its mobile fraud detection capabilities to better identify scam activity.

**Practical tip:** Ask your key software vendors whether they have been affected by any recently disclosed critical vulnerabilities, and confirm they have applied patches.

featured.title: Too Big to Fail – Hyperscale Data Centres Bring New Infrastructure Security Imperatives to Australia
featured.quote: Hyperscale data centres pose new security risks critical to Australia's national infrastructure.
